title: hpke.rs: recipient_secret_bytes stack copy not zeroized after PrivateKey::from_bytes
description: core/src/hpke.rs unseal_message(), lines 71-73: the caller passes recipient_secret_bytes as &[u8; 32]. Line 71 copies it into a Zeroizing<[u8;32]>. Line 72 calls PrivateKey::from_bytes(secret_zeroized.as_ref()), which copies the bytes again into the hpke crate's internal key representation. The Zeroizing wrapper clears secret_zeroized on drop, but the original caller's stack buffer (the *recipient_secret_bytes dereference) is never zeroed — it outlives the zeroization unless the caller explicitly zeroes it. The hpke crate's PrivateKey does not implement Zeroize (depends on version). If the caller stores the secret in a plain [u8;32] on the stack, it remains in stack memory after unseal_message returns. Severity: low (stack frame reused, not heap), but inconsistent with the zeroize intent.
